name: Rolling Hills Bank & Trust
description_short: Rolling Hills Bank & Trust is a community bank with 12 locations offering full-service banking, lending, and financial services. FDIC-insured with 150 years of local operation.
description_long:
Rolling Hills Bank & Trust originated as Anita State Bank in July 1876, making it a 150-year-old institution that has survived major economic downturns including the Great Depression and the 1980s savings-and-loan crisis. The bank is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Anita Bancorporation and operates as a traditional community bank with 12 convenient physical locations. The company positions itself as a "trusted hometown bank" focused on understanding the unique needs of local families, businesses, and individuals within their service area. Their longevity and stability are emphasized as core differentiators in a market increasingly dominated by larger national institutions.

Rolling Hills Bank & Trust offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including deposit products (checking, savings, CDs, Health Savings Accounts), lending products (real estate loans, commercial loans, agriculture loans), credit cards, online banking, and check reordering services. They provide both consumer and business financial solutions, with specialized lending expertise in agriculture through experienced local Ag Lending staff. Their online banking platform offers 24/7 account management capabilities, and they maintain FDIC insurance backing on deposits. The bank also supports account transitions for customers switching from other institutions, providing guidance on automatic payments, direct deposits, and fund transfers.

The bank distinguishes itself through hyperlocal decision-making authority—loan decisions are made locally rather than at a distant corporate headquarters. They emphasize personalized service through professionals who value individual and corporate customer needs. The 150-year heritage, survival through economic crises, and commitment to agricultural lending in what appears to be a rural or semi-rural area position them distinctly from national chain banks. Their job openings page and career focus suggest active community engagement and local employment.

The main limitation is lack of detailed product information on the website—interest rates, fees, APRs, minimum balances, and specific loan terms are not disclosed in available content. Customers would need to visit a branch or contact the bank directly for pricing details. As a regional bank with only 12 locations, geographic availability is restricted to their service area, limiting accessibility for remote customers. No mention of mobile app functionality or advanced digital features beyond basic online banking is evident. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with guaranteed returns
Health Savings Accounts (tax-free medical expense savings)
Real estate loans for home purchases and financing
Agriculture loans with local Ag Lending expertise
Commercial loans including equipment purchases and operating lines of credit
Credit cards for increased purchasing power
Online banking with 24/7 account access and management
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit backing from U.S. Government
150-year operating history demonstrates survival through Great Depression and 1980s financial crises
12 convenient physical locations for in-person banking and relationship management
Specialized agriculture lending with experienced local Ag Lending staff and local decision-making
Comprehensive product suite including real estate, commercial, and agriculture loans plus deposit products
24/7 online banking access for account management and monitoring
Full-service bank assistance for customers switching accounts from other institutions
Health Savings Accounts offering tax-free savings for medical expenses
cons: Website lacks specific pricing details—interest rates, APRs, fees, and minimum balances not disclosed
Limited geographic availability with only 12 branch locations restricts accessibility to service area
No evidence of mobile app or advanced digital banking features beyond basic online banking
Limited information about credit card rewards, benefits, or terms on website
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or trust services despite 'Trust' in company name
best_for: Individuals and families in the bank's service area seeking relationship-based community banking
Agricultural businesses and farmers requiring specialized local Ag lending expertise
Small to mid-sized commercial businesses needing equipment financing and operating lines of credit
Customers prioritizing in-person banking relationships and local decision-making authority
diagnosis: Rolling Hills Bank & Trust is best for individuals, families, and businesses within their 12-location service area who value traditional community banking, personalized relationships, and local decision-making. The primary caveat is that detailed product pricing and terms are not available online—customers must contact the bank directly or visit a branch to compare rates and fees with other institutions, and geographic service area limitations restrict accessibility for customers outside their region.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Rolling Hills Bank & Trust is a community bank with 12 locations, FDIC-insured, offering full-service banking and lending.
